1 · Exploratory Data Analysis

Click through the panels — each figure has a one-line statistical interpretation.

54.3% functional, 38.4% non-functional, only 7.3% need repair — a strongly imbalanced 3-class target.

Beyond true NaNs (scheme_name ~48%), 70% of amount_tsh and ~35% of construction_year/gps_height are placeholder zeros meaning 'unknown'.

amount_tsh is heavily right-skewed (log-transformed); gps_height is bimodal; construction_year peaks in the 1990s–2000s.

Failures cluster spatially by district — location carries strong signal.

Water quantity, waterpoint type, extraction class and payment type strongly separate the classes.

Functional probability falls from 74% (new) to 34% (25y+); dry pumps are almost all non-functional.

quantity (0.31), waterpoint_type (0.25), extraction_type_class (0.24), region (0.20) lead; all χ² p<0.001.

Grouped columns (waterpoint_type/…_group, quantity/…_group) are near-duplicates — one of each pair kept.

Numerics are mostly independent; only gps_height–coordinates show mild correlation.

Mutual information reproduces the χ² ranking; all 26 engineered features retain useful signal.

Association with target

Bias-corrected Cramér's V (all χ² p<0.001)

Model comparison

3-fold cross-validated accuracy vs macro-F1

2 · Model Selection & Tuning

Random Forest selected, then tuned on a held-out split.

Tree ensembles beat the linear baseline by ~14 pts; Random Forest is best.

Functional recall 0.91, non-functional 0.78; needs-repair only 0.30 (absorbed into majorities).

quantity #1, then longitude/latitude/gps_height, waterpoint_type and engineered age.

High precision across classes; recall gap concentrated in the rare needs-repair class.

Per-class performance (tuned RF)

Recall vs precision on hold-out

3 · Key findings

  • Water quantity is the strongest single driver — dry pumps are almost entirely non-functional.
  • Geography (region, longitude, latitude, gps_height) shows strong spatial clustering of failures.
  • Pump age degrades functionality monotonically: 74% functional when new → 34% past 25 years.
  • The tuned Random Forest reaches 81.5% accuracy; its weakness is the rare needs-repair class (recall 0.30).
  • Test-set predictions: 9,121 functional · 5,204 non-functional · 525 needs-repair.
